2604.00994.title: Multimodal Analysis of State-Funded News Coverage of the Israel-Hamas War on YouTube Shorts
2604.00994.paper_summary: YouTube Shorts have become central to news consumption on the platform, yet research on how geopolitical events are represented in this format remains limited. To address this gap, we present a multimodal pipeline that combines automatic transcription, aspect-based sentiment analysis (ABSA), and semantic scene classification. The pipeline is first assessed for feasibility and then applied to analyze short-form coverage of the Israel-Hamas war by state-funded outlets. Using over 2,300 conflict-related Shorts and more than 94,000 visual frames, we systematically examine war reporting across major international broadcasters. Our findings reveal that the sentiment expressed in transcripts regarding specific aspects differs across outlets and over time, whereas scene-type classifications reflect visual cues consistent with real-world events. Notably, smaller domain-adapted models outperform large transformers and even LLMs for sentiment analysis, underscoring the value of resource-efficient approaches for humanities research. The pipeline serves as a template for other short-form platforms, such as TikTok and Instagram, and demonstrates how multimodal methods, combined with qualitative interpretation, can characterize sentiment patterns and visual cues in algorithmically driven video environments.
2604.00994.paper_summary_zh: YouTube Shorts 已經成為該平台上新聞消費的核心，但關於地緣政治事件在這種格式中如何被表現的研究仍然有限。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了一個多模態流程，結合了自動轉錄、基於方面的情感分析（ABSA）和語義場景分類。該流程首先評估其可行性，然後應用於分析國家資助媒體對以色列-哈馬斯戰爭的短期報導。使用超過 2,300 個與衝突相關的 Shorts 和超過 94,000 幅視覺幀，我們系統地檢查主要國際廣播公司的戰爭報導。我們的研究結果顯示，關於特定方面的轉錄中表達的情感在不同媒體和時間上有所不同，而場景類型分類反映了與現實事件一致的視覺線索。值得注意的是，較小的領域適應模型在情感分析中表現優於大型Transformer甚至 LLM，突顯了資源高效方法在人文研究中的價值。該流程作為其他短格式平台（如 TikTok 和 Instagram）的模板，並展示了如何結合質性解釋的多模態方法，可以在算法驅動的視頻環境中表徵情感模式和視覺線索。
2604.00994.author: Daniel Miehling et.al.
